data.narrative:
Cohere is a Series D AI & Software company founded by Aidan Gomez, Ivan Zhang, and Nick Frosst, currently valued at $7.0B post-money following $1.6B in total capital raised. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 5 same-stage private companies in AI & Software (Wayve, Hugging Face, ElevenLabs, Hive AI, Labelbox) plus 1 public comparable (Palantir), representing the narrow but growing cohort of frontier-tech firms at this maturity level.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchored to the $7.0B last post-money valuation, applies a typical 1.5x step-up multiplier from Series D to subsequent funding rounds, implying a value of $10.5B. Peer-median analysis yields $3.0B, based on the median valuation across the 5-company peer set, which ranges from $1.0B (Labelbox) to $8.6B (Wayve). The dispersion reflects material variance in stage timing, capital efficiency, and revenue maturity within the AI sector.

Triangulation produces a range of $2.5B (low) to $12.1B (high), with a median implied value of $6.8B. Cohere's current $7.0B valuation sits near the triangulated median, suggesting alignment with stage and peer benchmarks. Key uncertainties include the timing and structure of Series E financing, comparative revenue traction versus peers, and the volatility inherent in early-stage AI company valuations amid rapidly shifting market expectations.
